executiveSummary.text: Regime: risk-on but inflation/real-yield constrained. Verified fresh inputs: S&P 500 7,563.63 (+0.58%), Nasdaq 26,917.47 (+0.91%), VIX 15.74 (-3.38%), Brent $92.35 (-0.38%), gold about $4,525 (-0.15%), STI 4,989.19 (-0.79%). Official Treasury 28 May: 10Y nominal 4.45%, 10Y real yield 2.06%. Scenario weights: 50% risk-on grind if oil stays below ~$95-$100 and real yields keep easing; 30% choppy multiple cap if inflation stays hot; 20% correlated drawdown if oil/PCE/Fed rhetoric lifts real yields again. Confidence: medium.

The Fed gate tightened: Yahoo Finance reported PCE rose 3.8% headline and 3.3% core in April, with officials still leaning steady-for-now but not ruling out hikes. First order: inflation is not solved. Second order: Fed cuts stay delayed and real yields remain the portfolio-wide discount-rate anchor. Third order: AI/QQQ, S-REITs and gold can fall together if real yields rise. Confidence: medium-high on policy constraint, medium on price path.

AI infrastructure demand remains thesis-confirming but increasingly crowded. Within the 24h window, Yahoo/Benzinga highlighted semis as a dominant S&P 500 driver, while Yahoo's Micron piece framed HBM/memory as an AI chokepoint. Probability: 55% durable AI-infra upcycle, 30% valuation chop from rates/crowding, 15% capex digestion. Confidence: medium.

Oil diplomacy moved from panic to relief but remains the macro switch. Yahoo reported US stocks hit records on US-Iran deal hopes while Brent traded around $93 and WTI slipped below $90; later Yahoo live strip showed Brent $92.35. Oil relief lowers inflation pressure (1st), reduces Fed/real-yield pressure (2nd), and supports duration assets/gold only if real yields fall (3rd). Confidence: medium.

Singapore had little fresh portfolio-specific corporate news inside the strict 24h window. Current quote context: STI 4,989.19 (-0.79%), DBS -0.11%, OCBC -0.86%. Treat SG exposure today as rate-channel exposure: banks may initially benefit from high rates, REITs remain pressured by refinancing/cap rates. Confidence: medium.

usGlobal.what: US stocks posted record closes: Yahoo Finance's live market report updated Fri 29 May 04:03 SGT said the S&P 500 gained 0.6%, Nasdaq 0.9%, and all three major averages hit records on US-Iran deal hopes and software/AI earnings strength.
usGlobal.why: First order: risk appetite is strong and volatility fell. Second order: lower oil pressure plus AI earnings keep liquidity flowing into long-duration growth. Third order: if inflation keeps the Fed steady/hawkish, the same long-duration winners can de-rate despite good fundamentals. Scenario weights: 50% grind higher, 30% range/chop, 20% sharp reversal. Confidence: medium.
usGlobal.implication: Stay invested, but do not chase fresh highs without real-yield confirmation.

usGlobal.what: Yahoo Finance reported April PCE at 3.8% headline and 3.3% core, with Fed officials still seeing policy as 'in a good place' while some remain prepared to raise rates if inflation persists.
usGlobal.why: First order: inflation remains above target. Second order: Fed cuts are delayed and real yields/cash remain competitive. Third order: QQQ/AI multiples, S-REIT cap rates, and gold can all be pressured together if real yields rise. Scenario weights: 45% steady high-for-longer, 30% inflation eases over coming months, 25% renewed hawkish repricing. Confidence: medium-high.
usGlobal.implication: The discount-rate gate matters more than headline AI enthusiasm today.

usGlobal.what: Yahoo Finance's Micron piece, published Thu 28 May 18:00 SGT, said Micron's rally has outpaced every major memory boom in three decades, with investors treating high-bandwidth memory as an AI buildout bottleneck.
usGlobal.why: First order: HBM/memory scarcity is being repriced. Second order: stronger memory demand pulls through foundry, packaging, power, and wafer-equipment demand. Third order: crowding risk rises if a cyclical memory industry is priced as permanently scarce. Scenario weights: 55% durable HBM upcycle, 30% crowded volatility, 15% cyclical disappointment. Confidence: medium.
usGlobal.implication: Scarcity thesis validated; add on pullbacks/order confirmation, not vertical moves.

usGlobal.what: Benzinga, published 28 May 09:01 ET, argued semiconductors now represent roughly 18% of the S&P 500 and have driven about 70% of the index's market-cap gains this year.
usGlobal.why: First order: broad index exposure now embeds a concentrated AI-chip bet. Second order: VWRA/QQQ diversification benefits weaken when one supply-chain theme drives returns. Third order: a semicap/AI unwind can hit both KL's index foundation and AI-infra sleeve simultaneously. Scenario weights: 50% concentration persists, 30% healthy earnings broadening, 20% concentration unwind. Confidence: medium.
usGlobal.implication: Monitor aggregate AI exposure across both foundation and risk buckets; not just single-name weights.

singapore.what: Yahoo Singapore's current quote page showed STI at 4,989.19, down 0.79% at the 5:20pm SGT close; DBS was shown at S$61.93 (-0.11%) and OCBC at S$23.16 (-0.86%) in the quote context.
singapore.why: First order: local equities lagged while global risk appetite improved. Second order: Singapore banks and REITs are being filtered through rates rather than local corporate news. Third order: if global real yields stay high, banks can hold up via NIM while REITs face valuation/refinancing pressure; if growth stress rises, bank credit risk can later offset NIM. Scenario weights: 55% ballast/lag, 30% banks outperform REITs, 15% broad Asia risk-off. Confidence: medium.
singapore.implication: No fresh local action; treat SG sleeve as income ballast and rate sensitivity, not a new catalyst.

commodities.what: Yahoo Finance's live market report said oil pared gains on US-Iran negotiation progress, with Brent around $93 and WTI below $90; Yahoo live strip later showed Brent $92.35 (-0.38%).
commodities.why: First order: lower oil reduces the immediate inflation impulse. Second order: Fed has less reason to tighten if oil stays contained, lowering real-yield pressure. Third order: AI/QQQ, REITs and gold benefit only if real yields fall; an inflationary oil spike would make cash more attractive and can hurt gold despite geopolitical stress. Scenario weights: 45% oil stays $90-$100, 35% renewed volatility, 20% break below $90 on diplomacy. Confidence: medium.
commodities.implication: Oil remains the macro switch; watch real yields, not crisis headlines.

commodities.what: Fresh commodity snippets showed gold near $4,500-$4,525 while Reuters/Brave noted gold gains were capped by rising bond yields and elevated crude; Yahoo live strip showed gold slightly lower around $4,525.
commodities.why: First order: gold is not simply responding to crisis risk. Second order: real yields near ~2.06% keep a high hurdle for a zero-yield asset. Third order: in an inflationary oil shock, Fed hawkishness can push real cash returns higher and gold lower. Scenario weights: 40% range-bound, 35% upside if real yields fall, 25% downside if PCE/oil lifts real yields. Confidence: high on framework, medium on price path.
commodities.implication: Do not add metals because 'geopolitical risk'; add only on real-rate/liquidity confirmation.

narratives.title: Risk-on rally is real, but the discount-rate gate still controls durability
narratives.description: US indexes closed at records while VIX fell, but hot PCE keeps Fed cuts delayed. Probability: 50% risk-on grind, 30% valuation cap, 20% drawdown if real yields rise. Confidence: medium.
narratives.source: Yahoo Finance live market report updated 2026-05-29 04:03 SGT; Yahoo PCE article updated 2026-05-29 00:26 SGT; US Treasury 2026-05-28 data
narratives.priceImpact: Positive while real yields fall; negative if PCE/oil reverses the yield easing.

narratives.title: HBM/memory is being repriced as AI scarcity infrastructure
narratives.description: Micron's rally is being framed as unprecedented because investors see HBM as a bottleneck, not just another memory cycle. Probability: 55% durable upcycle, 30% crowded volatility, 15% mean reversion. Confidence: medium.
narratives.source: Yahoo Finance Micron chart article published 2026-05-28 18:00 SGT
narratives.priceImpact: Fundamentally positive for scarcity basket; tactically risky after vertical moves.

narratives.title: Index investing is becoming more AI-chip concentrated
narratives.description: Fresh Benzinga article cited semiconductors at about 18% of the S&P 500 and roughly 70% of year-to-date market-cap gains. Probability: 50% concentration persists, 30% broadening, 20% concentration unwind. Confidence: medium.
narratives.priceImpact: Positive momentum while earnings validate; higher correlation risk across KL's foundation and AI-infra sleeves.

narratives.title: Oil relief is not the same as all-clear
narratives.description: US-Iran deal hopes pulled oil off stress levels, but the key chain remains oil -> inflation -> Fed -> real yields. Probability: 45% contained oil, 35% volatile mid-range, 20% renewed spike. Confidence: medium.
narratives.source: Yahoo Finance live market report updated 2026-05-29 04:03 SGT; Yahoo live commodity strip checked 2026-05-29 07:30 SGT
narratives.priceImpact: Positive for duration only if oil relief lowers real yields; negative if oil rebounds and Fed pricing tightens.

secondOrderEffects.text: Hot PCE -> Fed cuts delayed / possible hike optionality -> real yields stay competitive -> QQQ/AI multiples, S-REIT cap rates and gold face the same discount-rate headwind.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Do not assume diversification across AI, REITs and metals protects against a real-yield shock.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil relief -> lower inflation impulse -> lower Fed pressure -> real yields ease -> long-duration AI, REITs and gold can recover together.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Confirm with 10Y real yield after oil/PCE headlines before deploying cash.

secondOrderEffects.text: HBM scarcity -> memory pricing power -> higher capex/order pull-through for foundry/equipment -> potential crowding as investors chase every bottleneck.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Own the AI supply chain, but require order/backlog evidence and avoid FOMO adds.

secondOrderEffects.text: Semis dominate S&P gains -> index sleeve becomes less diversified -> AI-infra drawdown can hit both foundation and risk buckets at once.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Measure exposure by economic factor, not by account sleeve labels.

secondOrderEffects.text: High rates -> SG banks may benefit first through NIM -> REITs suffer cap-rate/refinancing pressure -> later growth stress can hurt bank credit quality.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Separate DBS/OCBC/UOB from S-REITs; do not treat SG dividends as one homogeneous defensive block.

investment.thesis: Maintain AI-infra scarcity exposure, but add only on pullbacks or rate confirmation.
investment.trigger: Add in small tranches if 10Y real yield holds below ~2.0%-2.1% and Brent stays below ~$95-$100, or if NVDA/AVGO/TSM/ASML/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/MU correct 8-12% without order/capex deterioration.
investment.whyNow: Fresh HBM/semiconductor concentration news confirms demand and scarcity, but crowding and real-yield risk are elevated.
investment.invalidator: AI capex cuts, HBM oversupply, semicap order weakness, Brent >$105-$110, or 10Y real yield >2.25%.

investment.thesis: Keep the cash barbell as strategic optionality toward the SGD 3M liquid FI target.
investment.trigger: Deploy more cash only after benign oil/PCE data shows up in lower real yields, or after thesis-intact risk assets sell off on positioning rather than fundamentals.
investment.whyNow: Cash still competes when real yields are ~2% and portfolio risk assets are increasingly correlated through AI/rates.
investment.invalidator: Sustained disinflation, falling real yields and broad earnings upgrades without oil shock.

trading.thesis: Tactical QQQ/AI adds require oil plus bond confirmation, not record-high headlines.
trading.trigger: Consider a small add only if Brent remains below ~$95-$100 for several sessions and the 10Y real yield continues lower after PCE/Fed commentary.
trading.whyNow: Indexes are at records and AI demand is strong, but hot PCE can reprice duration quickly.
trading.invalidator: Oil rebound, Fed hawkish repricing, or 10Y real yield back above ~2.2%.

trading.thesis: Gold trades through real yields, not crisis slogans.
trading.trigger: Add only if gold weakness coincides with falling real yields or genuine liquidity/trust stress, not an inflationary oil shock.
trading.whyNow: Gold was only modestly supported/capped despite geopolitics because real yields remain elevated.
trading.invalidator: Inflationary oil shock pushes Fed pricing and real yields higher.

watchlist.thesis: Memory/networking breadth is the next AI scarcity test.
watchlist.trigger: Watch whether Micron/HBM strength translates into AVGO, TSM, ASML, AMAT, LRCX and KLAC order/backlog commentary rather than just price momentum.
watchlist.whyNow: Investors are broadening from GPUs into HBM, networking, power and equipment bottlenecks.
watchlist.invalidator: No order/capex confirmation or signs of inventory pull-forward.

watchlist.thesis: Singapore REITs need benign rate relief, not recessionary cuts.
watchlist.trigger: Watch S-REITs only if real yields fall because inflation pressure eases; avoid if yields fall because credit/growth stress rises.
watchlist.whyNow: Fresh local catalysts are sparse and global rates dominate REIT valuation.
watchlist.invalidator: Refinancing spreads widen or cap rates rise despite lower headline yields.

riskWatch.name: PCE/Fed real-yield shock
riskWatch.description: Inflation at 3.8% headline and 3.3% core can keep Fed policy tight; rising real yields would pressure AI/QQQ, REITs and gold together.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep cash optionality; require real-yield confirmation before adding risk; do not buy gold solely on geopolitics.

riskWatch.name: AI-infra crowding
riskWatch.description: Semis driving a large share of index gains means thesis validation comes with concentration and drawdown risk.
riskWatch.mitigation: Own diversified bottlenecks; add on pullbacks/order evidence, not headlines.

riskWatch.name: Oil diplomacy reversal
riskWatch.description: US-Iran deal hopes lowered oil pressure, but a reversal would transmit through inflation, Fed pricing and real yields.
riskWatch.mitigation: Use Brent >$100 and 10Y real yield >~2.2% as caution triggers.

riskWatch.name: Singapore rate-channel divergence
riskWatch.description: Higher-for-longer can support bank NIM initially while pressuring REITs; later growth stress can hurt banks too.
riskWatch.mitigation: Separate bank ballast from REIT duration; avoid blanket SG-dividend adds.

keyEvents.text: Post-PCE Fed commentary: officials' reaction to 3.8% headline / 3.3% core PCE will drive rate-cut vs hike-optional pricing.
keyEvents.source: Yahoo Finance PCE article updated 2026-05-29 00:26 SGT

keyEvents.text: US-Iran/Hormuz negotiations: watch whether Brent stays near low-$90s or breaks back above $100.
keyEvents.source: Yahoo Finance live market report updated 2026-05-29 04:03 SGT

keyEvents.text: Next Treasury real-yield update: 10Y real yield was 2.06% on official 28 May data after falling 3bp day-on-day.
keyEvents.source: US Treasury nominal and real-yield XML

keyEvents.text: AI-infra breadth check: whether HBM/memory strength spills into foundry, equipment and networking backlog commentary.
keyEvents.source: Yahoo Finance Micron article and Benzinga semiconductor concentration article within 24h

bottomLine.whatMatters: The key chain is oil/PCE -> Fed pricing -> real yields -> AI multiples, S-REIT cap rates and gold. AI-infra demand still validates KL's scarcity thesis, but the discount-rate gate decides whether fundamentals are rewarded now.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold VWRA/QQQ/AI-infra and SG dividend ballast; keep cash optionality; add only after real yields confirm benign oil/PCE relief or after thesis-intact AI-infra pullbacks. Treat gold as a real-rate hedge, not a crisis trade.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Stale Singapore headlines, generic 'geopolitical risk = buy gold' shortcuts, and AI FOMO that ignores concentration, valuation and real-yield direction.
